skill.name: example-skill
skill.description: An example skill demonstrating the basic structure. Use when user asks about examples or templates.
skill.summary: An example skill demonstrating the basic structure. Use when user asks about examples or templates.
skill.author: ChaiWithJai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 3, but this is a false positive in plain frontmatter text. The reviewed file contains no cryptographic code, scripts, network calls, command execution, prompt injection text, or data exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3, but the line is a plain-text description of an example skill. No evidence found for cryptographic code, weak algorithms, command execution, network access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains only frontmatter description text. The full reviewed file has no code or references to cryptographic APIs.

content.value_statement: Creating a new AI skill can be unclear when the required structure is unfamiliar. This example gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users a minimal template for learning and iteration.
actual_capabilities: Shows a minimal SKILL.md frontmatter structure.
Defines simple principles for starting, testing, and iterating on a skill.
Offers an intake menu for understanding, creating, or debugging skills.
Provides a lightweight example for authors learning skill format basics.
Works as a reference for template-style skill conversations.
limitations: Does not include scripts, tools, or automated validation.
Does not provide advanced marketplace packaging guidance.
Does not execute commands or inspect project files by itself.
Provides general direction, not a complete skill development course.

use_cases.title: Learn Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Review a minimal skill file to understand frontmatter, principles, and intake sections.

use_cases.title: Start a Basic Skill Draft
use_cases.description: Use the example as a simple outline before adding domain-specific instructions.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot Early Skill Ideas
use_cases.description: Compare a draft against the simple principles for scope, testing, and iteration.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain what this example skill contains and how each section helps a new skill author.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a First Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me adapt this example into a simple skill for my topic. Keep the first version small.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Skill Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my draft skill against this example. Identify missing structure, unclear scope, and testing gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Skill Iteration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the principles in this example to create an iteration plan for improving my existing skill after user testing.

output_examples.input: Explain the structure of this example skill.
output_examples.output: The response describes the skill name and description, then explains the principles and intake options in simple terms.

output_examples.input: Help me create a first version of a writing helper skill.
output_examples.output: The response proposes a small first scope, clear principles, and an intake flow for common writing tasks.

output_examples.input: Debug why my skill feels too broad.
output_examples.output: The response points out broad goals, recommends a smaller starting scope, and suggests one test after each change.

best_practices: Start with one focused use case before adding more behavior.
Test the skill after each meaningful instruction change.
Update the skill based on observed failures and user questions.
anti_patterns: Adding broad instructions before the core workflow is clear.
Treating the example as a full production framework.
Skipping tests after changing the skill instructions.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It demonstrates a minimal skill structure for users learning how skills are organized.

faq.question: Does it run scripts or commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file contains only markdown instructions and frontmatter.

faq.question: Can I use it as a starting template?
faq.answer: Yes. It is useful as a simple starting point before adding topic-specific instructions.

faq.question: Is it suitable for advanced automation?
faq.answer: No. It does not include tools, scripts, or detailed automation workflows.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What security risk was reviewed?
faq.answer: A static warning on the description line was reviewed and found to be a false positive.
